name: Ascent to Roldan Peak and Puntal del Moco - Circular from El Portus
name_original: Ascenso al pico Roldán y Puntal del Moco — Circular desde El Portús
region: National Park Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso y Roldán
region_intro: Hiking around National Park Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso y Roldán offers diverse landscapes characterized by steep coastal mountain ranges and headlands that meet the Mediterranean Sea. The region features limestone formations, bays, cliffs, and small coves. Inland, Mediterranean forests with Aleppo pines and unique endemic species are present, alongside areas adapted to arid environments. This protected area provides varied terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around National Park Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso y Roldán The most popular hiking route is Cuestas del Cedacero - Peñas Blancas Loop, a 4.4 miles (7.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 23 minutes to complete. This moderate route provides panoramic views from the summit and insights into the area's mining history. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ascent to Roldan Peak and Puntal del Moco - Circular from El Portus, a moderate 5.8 miles (9.4 km) path. This trail involves a significant elevation gain, offering views of the entire Cartagena coastline. Local hikers also love the The Santa Barbara Ports Trail (Circular PR-MU 106), a 7.1 miles (11.5 km) trail leading through geological interest sites and cultural elements, often completed in about 3 hours 6 minutes. Hiking around National Park Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso y Roldán is defined by steep coastal mountain ranges, Mediterranean forests, and limestone cliffs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to challenging ascents. The routes in National Park Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso y Roldán are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore National Park Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso y Roldán's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in National Park Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso y Roldán?
A: The National Park Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso y Roldán offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 80 routes available. These range from easy strolls to more challenging ascents, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on the trails in this park?
A: You can expect diverse terrain characterized by steep coastal mountain ranges, headlands plunging into the Mediterranean Sea, and inland Mediterranean forests. The landscape features limestone formations, beautiful bays, cliffs, and small coves. Some areas also show evidence of mining history.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, the park offers 19 easy routes perfect for beginners or families. While specific family-friendly routes aren't detailed here, easier options like the "Cala Cerrada loop from Collado de Juan Catalina" mentioned in the region research provide accessible experiences. Many trails offer gentle gradients and shorter distances.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I visit while hiking?
A: The park is rich in historical and cultural sites. You can explore military architecture such as the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5058518" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batería de Castillitos&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4024514" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atalayón Battery&lt;/a&gt;. Ancient defense towers like the Torre de Santa Catalina and old mills also dot the landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e897661440/the-santa-barbara-ports-trail-circular-pr-mu-106" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Santa Barbara Ports Trail (Circular PR-MU 106)&lt;/a&gt;, for example, passes by geological interest sites and cultural elements like the Santa Bárbara flour mill.

Q: Where can I find the best panoramic views in the park?
A: For magnificent panoramic views, consider hiking to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6581220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas Blancas Summit&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2939367" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roldán Peak&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e899589280/cuestas-del-cedacero-penas-blancas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuestas del Cedacero - Peñas Blancas Loop&lt;/a&gt; provides excellent vistas from the summit, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e898475254/ascent-to-roldan-peak-and-puntal-del-moco-circular-from-el-portus" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Roldan Peak and Puntal del Moco - Circular from El Portus&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the entire Cartagena coastline. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3067287" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cedacero Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is another notable spot.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see in National Park Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso y Roldán?
A: The park is a designated Special Protection Area for Birds (ZEPA), so you might spot various bird species including golden eagles, Bonelli's eagles, eagle owls, peregrine falcons, and red-billed choughs. Mammals like foxes, wild cats, badgers, and wild boars also inhabit the park.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the park are circular. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e899589280/cuestas-del-cedacero-penas-blancas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuestas del Cedacero - Peñas Blancas Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e898475254/ascent-to-roldan-peak-and-puntal-del-moco-circular-from-el-portus" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Roldan Peak and Puntal del Moco - Circular from El Portus&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e897661440/the-santa-barbara-ports-trail-circular-pr-mu-106" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Santa Barbara Ports Trail (Circular PR-MU 106)&lt;/a&gt;. These loops allow you to experience diverse landscapes without retracing your steps.

Q: What unique plants can be found in the park?
A: The park is known for its rich flora, including typical Mediterranean forests with Aleppo pines, rosemary, and thyme. It's particularly renowned for unique endemic species such as the Cartagena cypress, Escombreras chamomile, Cartagena anacampsero, black garlic, and San Jose stems.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in National Park Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso y Roldán?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 500 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from steep coastal mountains to Mediterranean forests, and the variety of trails suitable for different ability levels. The stunning panoramic views and historical sites are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any natural monuments or unique features to explore?
A: Beyond the dramatic coastal cliffs and coves, you can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1462793" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Cañar&lt;/a&gt;, a spring that is particularly inviting on warmer days. The coastal fringe between Azohía and El Portús is notable for its limestone cliffs, including Cala Cerrada, Cala Salitrona, and the arch of Cabo Tiñoso.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in this region?
A: While hiking is possible year-round, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions with milder temperatures, making them ideal for exploring the park's diverse landscapes. Summers can be very hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended during that season.
tips: If you are in the Cartagena area you cannot miss the amazing views from the top of the Roldán. You can get there by several trails, some of them quite physically demanding to finally be rewarded with the best views!

A unique place with wonderful views. You can go up from El Portús on a short route or take a longer and more demanding route connecting with Pico Roldan. Either way you won't regret it, the views from either side are simply amazing.

waypoints.name: View of El Portús from Puntal del Moco
